creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to alt2600 from comment #2)
Guess what? I think the gnuplot dependency is a left over. I don't have any flavor of gnuplot in my system and I can build the documentation just fine. I think we can remove it altogether.

> https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/ports/ section 4.5.1 indicates I should be able to use ports...

And you can. With the limitations that exist when building in a "dirty" environment.

> I fail to see how working with html, sgml, and pdf requires a "clean environment".

Not that, but *building a package* (I never said compiling anything) might need a clean environment.

> Am I to understand that the freebsd-docs cannot or is not supported to be installed from ports on a system with anything but the most basic textproc/docproj ports installed?

No, I never said that either.

Can you confirm that you can build the documentation set without having gnuplot installed?
